completed_tasks.name: P1 — impact field injection callers
completed_tasks.detail: _all_callers_from_rg normalizes injects-edge FQNs via _normalize_owner_fqn. @Autowired/@Inject/@Resource/same-pkg all work.

completed_tasks.name: P2 — M3FiltroSeguridad/saint-server contamination removal
completed_tasks.detail: Purged from 8 source files. _CUSTOM_SECURITY_ANNOTATIONS and _CUSTOM_AOP_ANNOTATIONS are now empty extension-point dicts.

completed_tasks.name: P4 — cache status CWD-dependent
completed_tasks.detail: _resolve_repo_root walks .git ancestry; all cache status/clear/warm auto-resolve from any subdirectory.

completed_tasks.name: P8 — UX errors pr-impact and fix-bug
completed_tasks.detail: pr-impact --files error explains expected format. fix-bug detects symptom-as-path misuse.

completed_tasks.name: P6 — transactional count inconsistency (compact=33 vs spring-audit=178)
completed_tasks.detail: compact transactional_boundaries: count renamed to class_count (unique classes). spring-audit metadata: tx_boundaries_found renamed to tx_annotation_count (class+method annotations). tx_stats.class_level cross-references class_count. 3 tests updated.

completed_tasks.name: P7 — project_summary contaminated by doc text (e.g. Trivy output)
completed_tasks.detail: Added _TOOL_OUTPUT_RE to summarizer._extract_first_useful_paragraph. Rejects CVE ids, Trivy severity lines, scanner names (trivy/snyk/grype/syft), PURL identifiers, vulnerability-count patterns.

completed_tasks.name: P9 — repo-ir 71MB without warning
completed_tasks.detail: Added --gzip flag (requires --output, auto-appends .gz, shows compressed+uncompressed sizes). Added 10MB size warning for file output without --gzip. Added error when --gzip used without --output.

remaining_tasks.name: P3 — cache Windows slow (cold ~8s, warm ~4s)
remaining_tasks.note: Not reproducible on macOS (0.18s warm). Windows-specific: Path.replace() on locked files may fail silently -> CAS blobs: 0 -> no deduplication -> slower. Fix: _atomic_write in cache.py:810, add try/except PermissionError fallback for Windows.

blockers.description: P3 Windows cache requires Windows environment to reproduce and validate
blockers.workaround: Can inspect _atomic_write statically for Windows PermissionError patterns, but cannot validate fix without Windows

decisions.rationale: Agents comparing compact vs spring-audit saw contradictory numbers (33 vs 178). Labels now unambiguous without changing semantics.

decisions.decision: _TOOL_OUTPUT_RE added as separate class var (not folded into _LICENSE_MARKETING_RE)
decisions.rationale: Different category of noise; easier to extend independently.

decisions.decision: --gzip auto-appends .gz if output path doesn't end in .gz
decisions.rationale: Prevents silent binary corruption of .json files; user still controls filename.

decisions.decision: --gzip + stdout = error (not silent gzip to stdout)
decisions.rationale: Compressed binary on stdout breaks pipelines silently; explicit error is safer.

next_action: P3 only remaining — needs Windows env. Inspect cache.py:810 _atomic_write for PermissionError on Path.replace(), add fallback to shutil.copy2+unlink pattern.
context_notes: Session 85 complete. All 8 of 9 tasks done. P3 blocked on Windows. Tests: 2436 passed, 3 skipped, 0 new failures. Version still v1.35.30 (a17dcac base); P6/P7 at 2f0513f, P9 at 29a6c7d.
